observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor to continually oblige the former Municipal Treasurer to submit the accounts, reports and accounting records prior to August 2004 as basis to determine the correct balances of the accounts for the fair presentation of the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor oblige the former Municipal Treasurer to submit the accounts, reports and accounting records prior to August 2004 as basis to determine the correct balances of the accounts for the fair presentation of the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1. The Management sent thru a registered mail a follow-up letter dated July 6, 2021 to the former Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: The former Municipal Treasurer was unable to submit the accounts, reports and accounting records prior to August 2004 despite the effort exerted by the Office of the Municipal Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the relieved Municipal Treasurer (Mr. Leovino V. Ayuma) to deposit intact with authorized government depository banks (AGDBs) all undeposited collections of P2,330,440.28 as prescribed by Section 69(4) of PD No. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Management sent thru a registered mail a follow-up letter dated July 6, 2021 to the former Municipal Treasurer. The Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the relieved Municipal Treasurer (Mr. Leovino V. Ayuma) to deposit intact with authorized government depository banks (AGDBs) all undeposited collections of P2,330,440.28 as prescribed by Section 69(4) of PD No. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.reason: The former Municipal Treasurer was unable to deposit the collections from his incumbency. The Management was unable to receive any return/receipt card of their registered mail from the Post Office in their Municipality to support the receipt of the follow-up letter to the former Municipal Treasurer Leovino Ayuma.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor to continually oblige
the former Municipal Treasurer to submit the accounts, reports and accounting
records prior to August 2004 as basis to determine the correct balances of the
accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
 direct the General Services Officer Designate to complete the physical count
of all properties and the preparation of the RPCPPE in the required form with the
necessary information and data, and submit the same to the Audit Team
not later than January 31 of each year; and
 instruct the General Services Officer Designate and the Municipal Accountant
to reconcile their Property and Accounting Records, identify and investigate
the causes of the noted discrepancies, and effect the necessary adjustments
in both records, if warranted.
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to determine the details of the unaccounted PPEs amounting to
P4,598,044.68 by gathering relevant supporting documents available in the
Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to exert more efforts in retrieving records and documents such as
Check and Cash Disbursement Journals, General Journals and other documents
which were not submitted by the previous Municipal Accountants in order to
ascertain the real extent of their accountabilities.
We also reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
former Municipal Treasurer to submit the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting
documents and the Reports of Disbursements to facilitate reconciliation and to
be able to account, once and for all, the correct amount due from them.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the relieved
Municipal Treasurer (Mr. Leovino V. Ayuma) to deposit intact with authorized
government depository banks (AGDBs) all undeposited collections of
P2,330,440.28 as prescribed by Section 69(4) of PD No. 1445 and Section 32 of the
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
 direct the General Services Officer Designate to complete the physical count of all properties and the preparation of the RPCPPE in the required form with the necessary information and data, and submit the same to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year; and
 instruct the General Services Officer Designate and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their Property and Accounting Records, identify and investigate the causes of the noted discrepancies, and effect the necessary adjustments in both records, if warranted.
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine the details of the unaccounted PPEs amounting to P4,792,155.48 by gathering relevant supporting documents available in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The GSO was able to conduct a partial physical count of PPE with corresponding RPCPPE. The Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer Designate to complete the physical count of all properties and the preparation of the RPCPPE in the required form with the necessary information and data, and submit the same to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year. The Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer Designate and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their Property and Accounting Records, identify and investigate the causes of the noted discrepancies, and effect the necessary adjustments in both records, if warranted. Also, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine the details of the unaccounted PPEs amounting to P4,792,155.48 by gathering relevant supporting documents available in the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Municipality was unable to complete the physical count of properties acquired from 2018 and below due to insufficient fund of the GSO Office, not enough support of other offices for the inventory taking of the PPEs, lack of personnel and training and lack of storage facilities for equipment. No action had been undertaken by Management during CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts in retrieving records and documents such as Check and Cash Disbursement Journals, General Journals and other documents which were not submitted by the previous Municipal Accountants in order to ascertain the real extent of their accountabilities. We also reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the former Municipal Treasurer to submit the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents and the Reports of Disbursements to facilitate reconciliation and to be able to account, once and for all, the correct amount due from them.
observations.managementAction: The retrieval of records has been conducted. The Management sent thru a registered mail a follow-up letter dated July 6, 2021 to the former Municipal Treasurer. The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments for the affected accounts. The Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts in retrieving records and documents such as Check and Cash Disbursement Journals, General Journals and other documents which were not submitted by the previous Municipal Accountants in order to ascertain the real extent of the accountabilities of the former Municipal Treasurer and the Assistant Municipal Treasurer. The Management agreed to require the former Municipal Treasurer to submit the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents and the Reports of Disbursements to facilitate reconciliation and to be able to account, once and for all, the correct amount due from them.
observations.reason: The retrieval of records has been conducted. Most of the records were lost due to passage of time. The former Municipal Treasurer was unable to submit the Disbursement Voucher despite effort from Office of the Municipal Mayor. The observation was first raised in CY 2005 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
 a. General Services Officer Designate to:
  - maintain complete and updated stock cards per type of supply; and
  - prepare Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the original copy of Requisition and Issue Slip and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for the recognition of the expenses;
 b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC).
We also reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to record purchases of supplies/inventories initially under the appropriate asset account and recognize the expense once the inventories are consumed/distributed in accordance with IPSAS 12 for the fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements as required in Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Management commented that they maintain stock cards, SLC, SSMI. The Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to record purchases of supplies/inventories initially under the appropriate asset account and recognize the expense once the inventories are consumed/distributed in accordance with IPSAS 12 for the fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements as required in Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make a detailed review of the accounts and make the necessary adjustments for the fair presentation of these accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments for the fair presentation of the accounts. The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments for the fair accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor continually require all accountable officers and other municipal officials to liquidate their cash advances and other cash advances within the reglementary period. Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The accountable officers complied with the liquidation of unliquidated cash advances. The Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor continually require all accountable officers and other municipal officials to liquidate their cash advances and other cash advances within the reglementary period. The Municipal Accountant to send written notices or demands to the Accountable Officers to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
 a. file the request for write-off of uncollectible receivables with the Audit Team following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005; and
 b. require the Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the receivables accounts in the financial statements at net realizable amount in consistent with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan passed a Resolution No. 092-2020 dated October 1, 2020 to authorize the Municipal Mayor to file the request to write-off the uncollectible receivables and the request to write-off was filed with the Audit Team following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular on Audit. The Municipal Accountant set-up the necessary provision for Allowance for Impairment.
observations.reason: The Municipal Mayor was not able to file the request for write-off of these uncollectible accounts despite the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan thru Resolution No. 092-2020, thus rendering the account balance unreliable and affecting the fairness of its presentation in the financial statements which is not in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments for the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit. Henceforth, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The supporting documents were submitted to the Audit Team for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Accountant to verify the details of the unremitted amounts or the accumulated/unreconciled discrepancies in the Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts, and reconcile for their fair presentation in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1, and remit whatever balances due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: The reconciliation of records from available documents has been undertaken and still on-going for the identification of details for the account balances. The Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Accountant to verify the details of the unremitted amounts or the accumulated/unreconciled discrepancies in the Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts and reconcile the balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor: 
 the Sangguniang Bayan for the appropriation of funds under the General Fund necessary for the completion of CBMS; and
 the MPDC to determine the necessary personnel and equipment to be assigned/provided for the expeditious completion of CBMS that will establish/create a GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality maintains a manual collection of sex-disaggregated data of every department as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy. The Municipality is to establish/create a GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality. The Municipality informed that CBMS was not undertaken due to lack of funds and not enough personnel and equipment for the completion.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
 request the Sangguniang Bayan for the appropriation of funds under the General Fund necessary for the insurance coverage of properties with the GSIS; and
 require the Designated MGSO to determine the necessary personnel who will/can attend to the insurance coverage of properties of the Municipality in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction: Funds were allocated. The Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan for the appropriation of funds under the General Fund necessary for the insurance coverage of properties with the GSIS. The Management also agreed to require the Designated MGSO to determine the necessary personnel who will/can attend to the insurance coverage of properties of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Insurable properties were not yet covered with insurance. Not Insurable properties were still not covered with appropriate insurance due to non-appropriation of funds for the purpose under the General Fund for CY 2020 and lack of a dedicated personnel who can attend to this.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor oblige the former Municipal Treasurer to submit the accounts, reports and accounting records prior to August 2004 as basis to determine the correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The continuous disregard of the concerned accountable officers to submit the accounts, reports and accounting records prior to August 2004.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • direct the General Services Officer Designate to complete the physical count of all properties and the preparation of the RPCPPE in the required form with the necessary information and data, and submit the same to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year; and • instruct the General Services Officer Designate and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their Property and Accounting Records, identify and investigate the causes of the noted discrepancies, and effect the necessary adjustments in both records, if warranted. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine the details of the unaccounted PPEs amounting to P4,792,155.48 by gathering relevant supporting documents available in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: The conduct of inventory for PPEs purchased prior to CY 2019 was still not performed due to the lack of support from other offices in the Municipality as well as the incomplete turnover of the former GSO which he considered as a hindrance in completing the inventory. The skeletal workforce arrangement resulted to the limited time allotted for the conduct of the inventory.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts in retrieving records and documents such as Check and Cash Disbursement Journals, General Journals and other documents which were not submitted by the previous Municipal Accountants in order to ascertain the real extent of the accountabilities of the former Municipal Treasurer and the Assistant Municipal Treasurer. We also reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the former Municipal Treasurer to submit the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents and the Reports of Disbursements to facilitate reconciliation and to be able to account, once and for all, the correct amount due from them.
observations.reason: The accountable officers did not provide the documents needed to support the said disbursements in order to settle the receivables; records are no longer available due to passage of time.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. General Services Officer Designate to: • maintain complete and updated stock cards per type of supply; and • prepare Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the original copy of Requisition and Issue Slip and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for the recognition of the expenses; b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC). We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor monitor their compliance with the provision for storage room/stock room for the deliveries of inventories to be held in stock. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to record purchases of supplies/inventories initially under the appropriate asset account and recognize the expense once the inventories are consumed/ distributed in accordance with IPSAS 12 for the fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements as required in Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Failure to maintain complete and updated stock cards per office; SSMI not consistently prepared and submitted; provision for storage room not implemented as efforts concentrated on COVID-19 Pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make a detailed review of the accounts and make the appropriate adjustments for the fair presentation of these accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was not able to make the necessary adjustments; discrepancy arose from prepared/deducted IRA checks not yet transferred at year end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to send written notices or demands to the Accountable Officers to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor continually require all accountable officers and other municipal officials to liquidate their cash advances within reglementary period on a regular basis or as soon as the purpose of cash advance has been served, preferably upon completion of the project/program/activity. Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Long outstanding cash advances remain (including those of former Municipal Treasurer); while balances were reduced from prior year, P300,260.11 remained unliquidated at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • file the request for write-off of uncollectible receivables with the Audit Team following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular 2016-005; and • require the Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the receivables accounts in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Sangguniang Bayan passed Resolution No. 092-2020 authorizing filing for write-off but the Municipal Mayor has not yet filed the request; Municipal Accountant overlooked setting up allowance for impairment due to voluminous transactions.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the relieved Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact with authorized government depository banks (AGDBs) all undeposited collections of P2,333,873.16 as prescribed by Section 69(4) of PD No. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.reason: The relieved Municipal Treasurer was unable to deposit collections intact during incumbency; continuing non-deposit exposed funds to risk; recommended remedial actions not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to verify the details of the unremitted amounts of Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth, and to reconcile these accounts and remit whatever balances due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Retrieval of records and reconciliation from available documents has been undertaken and is still on-going; matching revealed under/over remittances; accounts remain unreliable pending full reconciliation and remittance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request/require: • the Sangguniang Bayan for the appropriation of funds under the General Fund necessary for the completion of CBMS; and • the MPDC to determine the necessary personnel and equipment to be assigned/provided for the expeditious completion of CBMS that will establish/create a GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: MPDC was not able to complete the CBMS due to lack of funds and insufficient personnel and equipment; sex-disaggregated data from other offices being used in the interim.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • request the Sangguniang Bayan for the appropriation of funds under the General Fund necessary for the insurance coverage of properties with the GSIS; and • require the Designated MGSO to determine the necessary personnel who will/can attend to the insurance coverage of properties of the Municipality in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and Republic Act No. 656.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Funds were not appropriated for insurance coverage; lack of a dedicated personnel to attend to insurance matters.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Mayor oblige the former Municipal Treasurer to submit the accounts, reports and
accounting records prior to August 2004 as basis to determine the correct
balances of the accounts for the fair presentation of the financial statements in
accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed that the
Municipal Mayor:
• direct the General Services Officer Designate to complete the physical count
of all properties and the preparation of the RPCPPE in the required form with
the necessary information and data, and submit the same to the Audit Team
not later than January 31 of each year; and
• instruct the General Services Officer Designate and the Municipal Accountant
to reconcile their Property and Accounting Records, identify and investigate
the causes of the noted discrepancies, and effect the necessary adjustments
in both records, if warranted.
Also, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine the details of the unaccounted
PPEs amounting to P4,792,155.48 by gathering relevant supporting documents
available in the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts in retrieving records
and documents such as Check and Cash Disbursement Journals, General
Journals and other documents which were not submitted by the previous
Municipal Accountants in order to ascertain the real extent of the accountabilities
of the former Municipal Treasurer and the Assistant Municipal Treasurer.
We also reiterated the recommendation and the Management agreed that the
Municipal Mayor require the former Municipal Treasurer to submit the
Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents and the Reports of
Disbursements to facilitate reconciliation and to be able to account, once and for
all, the correct amount due from them.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed that the
Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. General Services Officer Designate to:
• maintain complete and updated stock cards per type of supply; and
• prepare Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with
the original copy of Requisition and Issue Slip and submit the same to the
Municipal Accountant for the recognition of the expenses;
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC).
We also reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the
Municipal Mayor monitor their compliance with the provision for storage
room/stock room for the deliveries of inventories to be held in stock.
We further recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
require the Municipal Accountant to record purchases of supplies/inventories
initially under the appropriate asset account and recognize the expense once the
inventories are consumed/ distributed in accordance with IPSAS 12 for the fair
presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements as required in
Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require
the Municipal Accountant to make a detailed review of the accounts and make the
appropriate adjustments for the fair presentation of these accounts in the financial
statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to send written notices or demands to the
Accountable Officers to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
continually require all accountable officers and other municipal officials to
liquidate their cash advances within reglementary period on a regular basis or as
soon as the purpose of cash advance has been served, preferably upon completion
of the project/program/activity.
Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash
advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and
regulations.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
 a. file the request for write-off of uncollectible receivables with the Audit Team
following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular 2016-
005; and
 b. require the Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for
Impairment in order to present fairly the receivables accounts in the financial
statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of COA
Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the
financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Mayor require the relieved Municipal Treasurer (Mr. Leovino V. Ayuma) to deposit
intact with authorized government depository banks (AGDBs) all undeposited
collections of P2,330,440.28 as prescribed by Section 69(4) of PD No. 1445 and
Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct
the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the
correct balance of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require
the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting
documents for evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed
in audit.
Henceforth, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly
supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No.
1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor oblige the Municipal Treasurer to submit the accounts, reports and accounting records prior to August 2004 as basis to determine the correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The lack of cooperation among the management officials.
observations.reason: Reiterated in audit observation no. 1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the year so that the RPT/SET Receivables can be adjusted to reflect the correct balances as of December 31, 2019 for their fair presentation in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued a Memorandum dated September 1, 2020 to the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to address the above-cited observation and recommendations. The OIC-Municipal Treasurer replied that he submitted the Summary of Collectibles as of December 31, 2019 to the Municipal Accountant but acknowledged the need to prepare the summary of collectibles at the beginning and end of each year. The Municipal Accountant received the Summary and instructed the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit CY 2020 list.
observations.reason: The OIC-Municipal Treasurer was not able to submit the certified list of taxpayers. Reiterated in audit observation no. 4, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
 a. General Services Officer Designate to:
 • maintain complete and updated stock cards per type of supply; and
 • prepare Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the original copy of Requisition and Issue Slip and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for the recognition of the expenses;
 b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC).
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor continue to monitor their compliance with the provision for storage room/stock room for the deliveries of inventories to be held in stock.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued a Memorandum dated August 20, 2020 to the Municipal Accountant and the GSO Designate instructing them to comply with the Audit Team’s recommendations. The GSO Designate stated that an amount was allotted in the Annual Budget CY 2021 for the construction of a two-storey warehouse for the GSO. The Municipal Accountant committed to comply with maintenance of Supplies Ledger Cards for the current year.
observations.reason: Stock cards and SSMI were not being maintained; storage facility not yet provided. Reiterated in audit observation no. 5, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the relieved Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact with authorized government depository banks (AGDBs) all undeposited collections as prescribed by Section 69(4) of PD No. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor sent a follow-up letter dated September 8, 2020 addressed to the relieved Municipal Treasurer obliging him to deposit intact with authorized government depository banks all his undeposited collections. The said letter was copy furnished to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Relieved Municipal Treasurer has not deposited collections; follow-up letter was marked 'return to sender' and no action was received.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to expedite the completion of Community-Based Monitoring System that will establish/create a GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued a Memorandum dated September 1, 2020 to the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to address the above-cited observation and recommendations. The MPDC replied and committed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The MPDC was still unable to complete the CBMS; planned DILG training did not follow through. Reiterated in audit observation no. 10, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor request from Sangguniang Bayan for the appropriation of funds under the General Fund necessary for the payment of property insurance with the GSIS. Thereafter, require the General Services Officer Designate to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with the appropriate insurance in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and Republic Act No. 656.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued a Memorandum dated September 1, 2020 to the GSO Designate to address the observation and recommendations. The GSO Designate committed to take action on the processing of property insurance for all insurable properties once the necessary funds are available.
observations.reason: Funds were not appropriated; GSO Office not yet created; property insurance not processed due to lack of funds. Reiterated in audit observation no. 11, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor continually work for the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund through the implementation of the projects that will contribute to the development of the Municipality which will be beneficial to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued a Memorandum dated September 7, 2020 to the MPDC and Municipal Engineer to address the observation and recommendations.
observations.reason: Some projects were prioritized and bidding delays occurred; utilization rate remained low. Reiterated in audit observation no. 15, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor oblige the former
Municipal Treasurer to submit the accounts, reports and accounting records prior to August 2004 as basis to determine the correct balances of the accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• direct the General Services Officer Designate to complete the conduct of
physical count of all properties and the preparation of the RPCPPE in the
required form with the necessary information and data, and submit the same to
the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year; and
• instruct the General Services Officer Designate and the Municipal Accountant
to initiate reconciliation of the Property and Accounting Records to put their
records of PPE and accounts in agreement, identify and investigate the causes
of the noted discrepancies, and effect the necessary adjustments in both
records, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to exert more efforts in retrieving records and documents such as
Check and Cash Disbursement Journals, General Journals and other documents
which were not submitted by the previous Municipal Accountants in order to
ascertain the real extent of their accountabilities.
We also reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
former Municipal Treasurer and Assistant Municipal Treasurer to submit the
Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents and the Reports of
Disbursements to facilitate reconciliation and to be able to account, once and for
all, the correct amount due from them.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal
Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers
showing the amount due and collectible for the year so that the RPT/SET Receivables
can be adjusted to reflect the correct balances as of December 31, 2019 for their fair
presentation in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. General Services Officer Designate to:
• maintain complete and updated stock cards per type of supply; and
• prepare Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the
original copy of Requisition and Issue Slip and submit the same to the Municipal
Accountant for the recognition of the expenses;
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC).
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor continue to monitor their
compliance with the provision for storage room/stock room for the deliveries of
inventories to be held in stock.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
• require all the accountable officers to strictly comply with the provisions of COA
Circular No. 97-002 by immediately liquidating their cash advances and refund any
unexpended balance;
• instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated
at the end of the year; and
• instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to report the collection on the date it was
collected.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• request the Sangguniang Bayan for a Resolution to authorize him to file the
request for write-off of uncollectible receivables totaling P146,569.74 duly
supported by documents enumerated under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 16, 2016; and
• require the Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for
Impairment in order to present fairly the receivables accounts in the financial
statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of COA
Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the
financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the relieved
Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact with authorized government depository banks
(AGDBs) all undeposited collections as prescribed by Section 69(4) of PD No. 1445 and
Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts in retrieving records and documents such as Check and Cash Disbursement Journals, General Journals and other documents which were not submitted by the previous Municipal Accountants in order to ascertain the real extent of their accountabilities.
We also reiterated that the Municipal Mayor oblige the Municipal Treasurer and the previous Assistant Municipal Treasurer to submit the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents and the Reports of Disbursements to facilitate reconciliation and to be able to account, once and for all, the correct amount due from them.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor sent a follow-up letter to the relieved Municipal Treasurer obliging him to submit the Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents as well as the Reports of Disbursements. The said letter was copy furnished to the Audit Team. The Municipal Mayor issued a Memorandum to the Municipal Accountant to address the above-cited observation and recommendations.
observations.reason: The lack of cooperation among the management officials. Records are no longer available due to passage of time. Reiterated in audit observation no. 3, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO Designate to prepare immediately the pertinent inventory reports. Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers and property cards respectively, for all types of properties and reconcile the same to establish the correct PPE balances and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that is noted.
observations.managementAction: The GSO Designate was able to conduct an inventory of some PPEs and submitted an inventory sheet to the Audit Team, however it was not in the prescribed form. The Municipal Accountant was able to maintain the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for all the PPEs.
observations.reason: Inventory sheet submitted was not in the prescribed form and PPEs were not classified/grouped as required. Reiterated in audit observation no. 2, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts in retrieving records and documents such as Check and Cash Disbursement Journals, General Journals and other documents which were not submitted by the previous Municipal Accountants in order to ascertain the real extent of their accountabilities.
We also reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the former Municipal Treasurer and Assistant Municipal Treasurer to submit the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents and the Reports of Disbursements to facilitate reconciliation and to be able to account, once and for all, the correct amount due from them.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor sent a follow-up letter dated September 8, 2020 addressed to the relieved Municipal Treasurer obliging him to submit the Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents as well as the Reports of Disbursements. The said letter was copy furnished to the Audit Team on September 8, 2020. The Municipal Mayor issued a Memorandum dated September 7, 2020 to the Municipal Accountant to address the above-cited observation and recommendations.
observations.reason: Records no longer available due to passage of time and continued disregard by concerned accountable officers to provide documents. Reiterated in audit observation no. 3, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
 • require all the accountable officers to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 by immediately liquidating their cash advances and refund any unexpended balance;
 • instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year; and
 • instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to report the collection on the date it was collected.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued a Memorandum dated September 1, 2020 to the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to address the above-cited observation and recommendations. The Municipal Accountant provided the list of accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances as of year-end and committed to strict implementation of guidelines on cash advances.
observations.reason: Most outstanding cash advances were liquidated as of December 31, 2019 but some remained unliquidated and some refunds were not reported in the year collected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 • request the Sangguniang Bayan for a Resolution to authorize him to file the request for write-off of uncollectible receivables totaling P146,569.74 duly supported by documents enumerated under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016; and
 • require the Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the receivables accounts in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued a Memorandum dated August 27, 2020 to the Municipal Accountant instructing to set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment. The Municipal Mayor also sent a letter dated August 27, 2020 to the Sangguniang Bayan informing them of the observations. The Municipal Accountant committed to set-up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in CY 2020 financial statements.
observations.reason: Written authority from Sangguniang Bayan to file write-off request not yet obtained; provision for impairment to be set up in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
 • Municipal Accountant regularly monitor the available cash and to set aside the cash pertaining to trust collections/receipts; and
 • OIC-Municipal Treasurer to adopt strategies to intensify collections that would improve the financial viability of the Municipality; and
 • Municipal Budget Officer to use the Advice of Allotment (AA) in releasing the allotment to serve as guide; and
 • adopt austerity measures.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Treasurer adopted strategies to intensify collections. The Municipal Accountant regularly monitor the available cash. The Advice of Allotment is being used and austerity measures are being observed by the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Municipality is observing the recommended measures and improved monitoring/collection strategies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the schedule which will serve as basis for the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year for submission to the Audit Team. Likewise, submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued a Memorandum dated September 7, 2020 to the Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit the schedules and monthly monitoring reports.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer complied by preparing schedules of list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and submitting the monthly monitoring reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRM Officer to properly identify and categorize programs, projects and activities that are to be charged to MDRRMF and to utilize the fund strictly for calamity related activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality complied with the MDRRMF program activities to be charged to MDRRMF and ensured utilization for calamity related activities.
observations.reason: The MDRRMF PPAs were properly identified and charged to MDRRMF. The Municipality realized its targeted projects for the year.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor oblige the Municipal
Treasurer to submit the accounts, reports and accounting records prior to August 2004
as basis to determine the correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in retrieving records and documents such as
Check and Cash Disbursement Journals, General Journals and other documents which
were not submitted by the previous Municipal Accountants in order to ascertain the real
extent of their accountabilities.

We also reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Mayor oblige the
Municipal Treasurer and the previous Assistant Municipal Treasurer to submit the
Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents and the Reports of
Disbursements to facilitate reconciliation and to be able to account, once and for
all, the correct amount due from them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO Designate to
prepare and submit immediately the pertinent inventory reports. Furthermore,
require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO Designate to prepare the
corresponding subsidiary ledgers and property cards, respectively, for all types of
properties and reconcile the same to establish the correct PPE balances and to
investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. GSO Designate to:
 maintain complete stock cards per type of supply; and
 submit to the Municipal Accountant the Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued together with the original copy of Requisition and
Issue Slip
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards
(SLC)

We also recommended that provision for storage room/stock room be
considered in the Municipal Hall for deliveries of inventories to be held in stock.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 require all the accountable officers to strictly comply with the provisions
of COA Circular No. 97-002 by immediately liquidating their cash
advances and refund any unexpended balance;
 instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are
fully liquidated at the end of the year and to allow the granting of
succeeding cash advance only when the previous one is fully liquidated;
and
 instruct the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to withhold the payment of any
money due to the concerned accountable officers with long outstanding
cash advances.

Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash
advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and
regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the current OIC - Municipal
Treasurer to submit to the current Municipal Accountant the certified list of
taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the year so that the
RPT/SET Receivables can be adjusted to reflect the correct balances as of
December 31, 2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the current Municipal
Accountant to make a detailed review of the accounts to determine the causes
of the unreconciled balances and provide the necessary adjustments in the
books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Accountant and OIC - Municipal Treasurer to revert to the
Unappropriated Surplus the unaccounted balance of Due to NGAs account of
P230,240.42 and submit to the Audit Team the proof of the liquidation of the
unused balance of DOH - TB DOTS of P470.09.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the:
 Sangguniang Bayan for a Resolution to authorize him to file the request
for write-off of uncollectible receivables of P146,569.74; and
 Municipal Accountant to support the request for authority to write-off
uncollectible receivables following the guidelines and procedures
provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the relieved
Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact with authorized government depository banks
(AGDBs) all undeposited collections as prescribed by Section 69(4) of PD No. 1445
and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:
a) Municipal Accountant to regularly monitor the available cash and to set
aside the cash pertaining to trust collections/receipts;
b) OIC - Municipal Treasurer to adopt strategies to intensify collections that
would improve the financial viability of the Municipality; and
c) the Municipal Budget Officer to use the Advice of Allotment (AA) in
releasing the allotment to serve as guide of concerned office/s as to the
maximum amount it could spend for the quarter and to coordinate with the
Municipal Accountant and OIC - Municipal Treasurer to determine the
financial capability of the Municipality to ensure that the issued AAs are
covered by available cash and/or future collections to avoid incurrence
of cash deficit.

We further recommended to adopt austerity measures in order to reduce/trim
down the expenditures so as not to hamper the services and priority projects
intended for the constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to
prepare the schedule which will serve as basis for the list of all on-going
government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be
implemented during the year for submission to the Audit Team. Likewise, submit
the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the existing regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer to conduct physical inventory of properties and ensure that report thereon is prepared and submitted.

Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers and property cards, respectively, for all types of properties and reconcile the same to establish the correct PPE balances and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.
observations.reason:
The GSO Designate was not able to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.
Not Implemented With this new development, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew in Audit Observation No. 3, Page No. 32

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivables with the Taxpayer’s Index Card and the Due from Other Funds with its reciprocal account Due to Other Funds and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason:
The previous Municipal Accountant was able to effect necessary adjustments for the RPT and SET receivables; however the Due from/Due to Other Funds reciprocal accounts remain unreconciled since the current Municipal Accountant assumed office only in December 2018 and has yet to perform a detailed review to verify the causes of the discrepancy.
With this new development, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew in Audit Observation No. 7, Page No. 39

prior to August 2004 as basis to determine the correct balances of the accounts in
the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC —
Municipal Accountant to continue her efforts in retrieving records and
documents such as Check and Cash Disbursement Journals, General Journals
and other documents which were not submitted by the previous Municipal
Accountants in order to ascertain the real extent of their accountabilities.

We also reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Mayor oblige the
Municipal Treasurer and the previous Assistant Municipal Treasurer to submit the
Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents and the Reports of
Disbursements to facilitate reconciliation to be able to account, once and for all,
the correct amount due from them.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact with authorized government depository
banks (AGDBs) all collections as prescribed by Section 69(4) of Presidential
Decree No. 1445 and Section 32, Volume I of the Manual on New Government
Accounting System.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor immediately comply
with the directive of the Officer-in-Charge — Regional Director, Commission on
Audit on the immediate relief of Mr. Ayuma from his duties as the Municipal
Treasurer and render a written explanation on the failure/delay of his action.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the General
Services Officer to prioritize the conduct of the physical inventory of
properties and ensure that report thereon is prepared and submitted,

Furthermore, require the OIC — Municipal Accountant and General
Services Officer to prepare the corresponding ledger cards and property cards for
all types of properties and reconcile the same to establish the correct PPE balances
and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

* require all the accountable officers to liquidate immediately their
cash advances;

* instruct the OIC — Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash
advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year and to allow the
granting of succeeding cash advance only when the previous one is
fully liquidated; and

e submit the liquidation report on the cash advance for intelligence
expenses amounting to 2103,000.00 to the Intelligence and
Confidential Fund Audit Unit (ICFAU), Office of the Chairman,
Commission on Audit

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Treasurer to submit to the OIC — Municipal Accountant the certified
list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the year so that the
RPT/SET Receivables can be adjusted to reflect the correct balances as of
December 31, 2016.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a) Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to:

e maintain complete stock cards per type of supply
¢ submit to the OIC — Municipal Accountant the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued together with the original copy of
Requisition Issue Slip

b) OIC — Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies
Ledger Cards

We recommended that the Municipality request for write-off of the
uncollectible receivables totaling P146,569.74 from the Commission on Audit in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016,

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer to submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/
activities (PPA) and those that are to be implemented during the year. Likewise,
submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above-cited
regulations.

We recommended the full utilization of the 20% Development Fund
through the implementation of the projects that will contribute to the
development of the Municipality which will be beneficial to its constituents.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the accountable
officer concerned to liquidate immediately the cash advance for Sports Program.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned
employees to immediately refund the amount of 215,204.22 representing excessive
and irregular disbursements,

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned
employees to immediately refund the amount of P4,316.45. Henceforth, require
the OIC — Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit the GSIS
contributions/premiums in compliance with the aforecited regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Treasurer and the OIC — Municipal Accountant to remit immediately
to the Provincial Government of Quezon and to the six barangays the year-end
balance of £419,593.10.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC — Municipal
Accountant to reclassify the amount of £122,908.75 from the Due to LGUs account
to the Due to NGAs account, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

o provide the necessary support in enjoining the barangays to establish
their own MRF and to strictly implement waste segregation;

o develop and operate a sanitary landfill as final disposal site for wastes
of the Municipality; and

e strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines
under RA No. 9003.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require

a) the OIC - Municipal Accountant to regularly monitor the available cash and to set aside the cash pertaining to trust collections/receipts;

b) the Municipal Treasurer to adopt strategies to intensify collections that would improve the financial viability of the Municipality; and

c) the Municipal Budget Officer to use the Advice of Allotment (AA) in releasing the allotment to serve as guide of concerned office/s as to the maximum amount it could spend for the quarter and to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to determine the financial capability of the Municipality to ensure that the issued AAs are covered by available cash and/or future collections to avoid incurrence of cash deficit.

We further recommended to adopt austerity measures in order to reduce/trim down the expenditures so as not to hamper the services and priority projects intended for the constituients.
observations.managementAction:
The concerned municipal officials partially
complied with the
recommendations.
observations.reason:
The  Municipality  still
incurred cash overdraft but
was reduced to
£6,496,463.95 as of

December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials to stop the practice of transferring cash advance from one accountable officer to another, in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality complied with recommendation to stop the practice of transferring cash advance among accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to ensure that the Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality are insured with the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS).
observations.managementAction:
The municipal ambulance
and backhoe were insured
with  the  Government
Service Insurance System
(GSIS).
observations.reason:
Other properties of the
Municipality still remain
uninsured due to lack of
funds

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete documents are attached to each claims for payment in compliance with the provisions of Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 138 of GAAM Volume I and submit the documents
observations.managementAction:
Ensured that DVs are
supported with proper and
complete documentations.

Some documents were
submitted to the Audit
Team. With issued Notice |
of Suspensions totaling to
